title: baeria minor subsp. maritima
intro: Baeria minor subsp. maritima, also known as Baeria minor maritime, is a subspecies of Baeria minor, a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a small, annual or biennial plant native to coastal areas of Europe and North Africa, characterized by its small yellow flowers and distinctive leaf morphology. Traditionally, Baeria minor subsp. maritima has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which are believed to contribute to its potential health benefits, such as wound healing and immune system support. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a dried herb or infusate, and can be consumed as a tea or added to topical creams and ointments.
